mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which generates positive short-term market sentiment and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day return direction with an accuracy rate above 52% in early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive federal funds rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of high CPIAUCSL and high FEDFUNDS yields positive 20-day returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of declining real gross domestic product confirm a technical recession, triggering capital flight from equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 50% of confirmed recession onset periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and total payrolls signal synchronized supply-side and labor market expansion, boosting near-term market momentum and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline over 5-day horizons with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 in walk-forward tests.
